# LLD ‚Äî epic-suggester

<!-- generated by /lld v2.21.0 on 2026-05-29; promoted to canonical from docs/shield/backlog-20260527/ on 2026-05-29 -->

**Feature:** `backlog-20260527`
**Owner:** `ashwini.manoj@aspora.com`
**Status:** `promoted`
**Linked PRD:** [`prd.md`](../shield/backlog-20260527/prd.md)
**Linked plans:** [`plan.md`](../shield/backlog-20260527/plan.md)
**Version:** `0.1.0`
**Last updated:** `2026-05-29`

## ¬ß1 Overview {#overview}

`epic-suggester` is a **read-only** Python helper that, at capture/promotion time, proposes a
matching feature (from `manifest.json`) and epic (from that feature's `plan.json`) for a backlog
entry. It serves PRD milestone **M2** and TRD [¬ß5 F7](../shield/backlog-20260527/trd.md#functional-requirements)
(exact-normalized match). Story EPIC-2-S2 touches this component. It never writes ‚Äî it returns
candidates; the user accepts/replaces/creates-new and `backlog-store` persists the choice.

## ¬ß2 Scope & non-goals {#scope-and-non-goals}

**In scope:** scanning `manifest.json` features and a candidate feature's `plan.json` epics;
exact-normalized matching (`casefold()` + collapsed whitespace); returning ranked-by-presence
candidates and surfacing ties.

**Out of scope:** fuzzy / token-overlap ranking (explicitly excluded in v1); writing the
association (owned by `backlog-store`); the removal decision (owned by `reconciler`, which uses
the same normalization but a different question ‚Äî "did the epic land?").

## ¬ß3 Module layout {#module-layout}

```
shield/scripts/
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ epic_suggester.py        new   (normalize + suggest_feature + suggest_epic)
```

Reads (never writes): `docs/shield/manifest.json`, `docs/shield/<feature>/plan.json`.

## ¬ß4 Data model {#data-model}

n/a ‚Äî stateless helper, no persistent data model. Operates on already-read `manifest.json` and
`plan.json` documents passed in (or read on demand) and returns in-memory candidate lists.

## ¬ß5 API contracts {#api-contracts}

#### normalize() {#api-normalize}

`normalize(name: str) -> str` ‚Äî `casefold()` + collapse internal whitespace to single spaces +
strip. The single normalization used by both suggestion and (independently) the reconciler, so a
proposed-new name suggested here matches the name the reconciler later gates on.

#### suggest_feature() {#api-suggest-feature}

`suggest_feature(text: str, *, manifest: dict) -> list[str]` ‚Äî reads `manifest["features"][].name`
(the manifest is a list keyed by `name`) and returns the names whose normalized form appears in
the normalized capture text. **`features[].name` IS the feature folder slug** (invariant pinned in
`backlog-store` LLD / EPIC-1-S1), so a returned value resolves directly to `docs/shield/<value>/`
‚Äî this is the reconciliation key the `reconciler` later derives the plan path from. Empty list ‚áí
no match (capture proceeds proposed-new).

#### suggest_epic() {#api-suggest-epic}

`suggest_epic(text: str, *, feature: str, plans: dict[str, dict]) -> list[Candidate]` ‚Äî `plans`
is a `{feature-slug ‚Üí parsed plan.json}` map; reads `plans[feature]["epics"][]` and returns
candidates by normalized-**name** presence. A `Candidate` carries `{epic_id, name, match_kind}`
where `epic_id` is informational only (the within-plan `EPIC-N` slot) ‚Äî matching and the later
removal gate (F8) key off `name`, never the positional id. A tie (‚â•2 candidates) is returned in
full; the caller auto-picks none.

## ¬ß6 Sequence flows {#sequence-flows}

#### Suggest on capture {#flow-suggest}

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant C as /backlog add
  participant E as epic_suggester
  participant FS as manifest.json / plan.json
  C->>E: suggest_feature(text, manifest)
  E->>FS: read manifest features
  E-->>C: [feature candidates]  (possibly empty)
  C->>E: suggest_epic(text, feature, plans)
  E->>FS: read feature's plan.json epics
  E-->>C: [epic candidates]  (ties surfaced, none auto-picked)
  C->>C: user accepts / replaces / creates-new
```

## ¬ß7 Error handling {#error-handling}

| Error | Raised when | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| (none ‚Äî soft) | `manifest.json` / `plan.json` missing or unreadable | return empty candidate list (capture proceeds proposed-new); log a debug line. Suggestion never blocks capture (F7). |
| malformed plan shape | `plan.json` lacks `epics[]` | treat as no candidates; log debug; do not raise |

## ¬ß8 Concurrency & state {#concurrency-and-state}

n/a ‚Äî stateless, read-only helper, no concurrency-sensitive state. It reads point-in-time copies
of `manifest.json`/`plan.json`; staleness is harmless (the user confirms the choice, and the
reconciler re-reads at removal time).

<details>
<summary>¬ß9 Configuration {#configuration}</summary>

n/a ‚Äî no configuration. Normalization rules are fixed (F7).

</details>

## ¬ß10 Observability {#observability}

- **Logs:** a debug line per suggestion `{text_excerpt, feature_candidates, epic_candidates}`; a debug line when inputs are missing/malformed (‚Üí empty candidates).
- **Metrics:** none in v1. The PRD ¬ß7 "suggestion acceptance ‚â•60%" is tracked manually via the `/backlog` audit, not telemetry.
- **Traces:** n/a ‚Äî single in-process call.

<details>
<summary>¬ß11 Security & privacy {#security-and-privacy}</summary>

n/a ‚Äî reads developer-authored project artifacts only; no user data, auth, or PII.

</details>

## ¬ß12 Performance & scaling {#performance-and-scaling}

#### ¬ß12.1 Load {#load}
Invoked once per capture/promotion (interactive, low frequency).

#### ¬ß12.2 SLO {#slo}
Returns candidates in ‚â™ 100ms; contributes to the TRD ¬ß6 N2 ~1s budget when run during capture.

#### ¬ß12.3 Bottleneck {#bottleneck}
IO-bound: reading `manifest.json` + one `plan.json`. Matching is linear over a handful of epics.

#### ¬ß12.4 Latency breakdown {#latency-breakdown}
Two small file reads dominate; normalization + comparison is negligible. `n/a ‚Äî finer numbers measured post-ship`.

#### ¬ß12.5 Capacity {#capacity}
Bounded by feature count (manifest) √ó epics-per-plan ‚Äî tens, not thousands. Trivial memory.

#### ¬ß12.6 Scale-out lever {#scale-out-lever}
n/a ‚Äî local read-only helper; no scale-out dimension. If manifest scanning ever dominates the N2 budget, the ¬ß8/¬ß12 mitigation is the project-level epic index (TRD ¬ß8 alt 4 / ¬ß12 Q1).

#### ¬ß12.7 Caches {#caches}
None in v1 ‚Äî reads fresh each call. A read cache is the lever if N2 is breached (deferred).

#### ¬ß12.8 Degradation {#degradation}
On any missing/malformed input, degrades to "no candidates" ‚Üí capture proceeds proposed-new. Suggestion failure never blocks the user.
